## [00:00 - 03:00]

Well, welcome everybody. This is Mark Pasha and we have with us the faculty of Robin's Bonanus Training, Tony Robbins, Chloe Medanus, Magna Pasha and myself. And we're going to be talking about some of the opportunities that are alive right now in the field of coaching. It's a very exciting time to be a coach. It's a time when the mainstream public is really embracing the idea that someone could come into their lives and help them take steps and actions to make a significant improvement in their life. So, we'll discuss some of the opportunities as well as some of the myths, some of the preconceptions about coaching that are holding coaches back. And then we'll talk about how smart people from a variety of different career backgrounds and different industries are using the skills of coaching, not just in coaching sessions with clients, but also in the workplace to better understand and help the people that they live and work with. So, today's conversation will focus on three topics. One, the role of emotions in coaching. Two, the goal of the coach. And three, the new applications of coaching skills in a variety of industries. So, Tony, I want to say welcome. Thank you. It's great to be with the family here, even though we're a bit virtual right now in different parts of the world. So, thanks for everybody on. Great. So, our first topic is about the role of emotion in coaching. Behind every objective-driven, focused person is an emotional person as well. And so, whether coaches have clients coming in about emotional problems or they're coming in with very objective goal-driven solutions in mind, as coaches, we always keep in mind the emotions that people are using and how they can gain more power through healthy and productive emotional states. So, Tony, you teach that most of what holds someone back, both in their achievements as well as their ability to enjoy their achievements, is their psychology. So, their ability to create meaning and emotion. So, let's talk about how you use emotion to create change. I think this is one of the single largest challenges that hold coaches back. I think so many of us are driven by our psychology, our, I should say, our minds rather, our intellect. And we want to know all the strategies and all the tools and all the answers to do something. And you know me very well. I'm, I'm insanely obsessed with finding strategies because strategies can compress decades into days. If you figure a better way to do something, you can save somebody who's been hammering their head against the wall an enormous amount of time. But what I've found is all, all real change, somebody can have the right strategy sitting right in front of them. You can show them what to do, how to do it. They know exactly what to do and they're still not doing it. And the only reason is because the emotional content that's going on. I always tell people emotion is the invisible force. It is the force that changes people, including the people that act like they have no emotion because they're hanging on by a thin thread underneath because of what they really feel. They're afraid to feel. And so, you think about it as an invisible force. What affects our lives the most? Are there invisible forces? I don't care if it's gravity or radiation or emotion, which, what has more power? Love and hate have about as much power as anything on earth.

Emotion is what starts a war. Emotion is the only way you're going to, a change in emotion is going to create peace. ## [06:02 - 09:03]

You know that today there's great interest in psychology and philosophy circles and the issue of happiness and how to achieve it. There's even courses being taught at universities on how to be happy. If you believe that happiness comes from living with passion. Well, I think, you know, Chloe, you and I, I have so much love and respect for you because you live this world of meaning. Let me clarify. I think happiness is the mental game that people are pursuing and it doesn't last. Think of it this way. Pleasures in the body. Happiness is in the mind. Joy is in the spirit. So what I think people are really after is an extraordinary quality of life. They want an extraordinary quality of life, which would be life on their terms, which can be defined by anybody because one person's idea of life on my terms is to have this beautiful garden. Somebody else wants to build a building dollar company. Someone else wants 2.3 perfect children. Somebody else wants to merge with God. Like your model of the world determines to a good extent how you feel. When people are happy as a rule in their mind, it's because their life conditions match how they think it should be. If I ask people all the time, I'll say write down for me an area of your life. I don't care how unhappy you are normally. Just what's an area of your life that you're pleased with? What's an area of your life that, sure you may want more, but you really do feel good about that area of your life. I don't care about your body or your emotions or your relationship or your finances or the relationship of your kids or relationship with God. And a lot of people come up with a little bit of that area. And I'll say then write down why you're happy with that area of your life.
